description: Kavach scam-tactic taxonomy. Each tactic is a class the on-device intent classifier predicts per transcript window. `cues` seed the reference detector and the labeling guide; `weight` feeds risk fusion; `explanation` is the pre-vetted, plain-language line shown to the user (never LLM-generated).

tactics.name: Manufactured urgency
tactics.explanation: This caller is rushing you. Real family and real institutions let you take your time.
tactics.action: Slow down. Hang up and call back on a number you already trust.

tactics.name: Secrecy demand
tactics.explanation: This caller wants you to keep it secret. Scammers isolate you so no one can warn you.
tactics.action: Tell someone you trust right now. Secrecy is a red flag, not a favor.

tactics.name: Untraceable-payment request
tactics.explanation: This caller wants gift cards, crypto, or a wire transfer. No real agency or family member is ever paid this way.
tactics.action: Stop. Do not buy or send anything. This is how scammers take money they can't be forced to return.

tactics.name: Authority impersonation
tactics.explanation: This caller claims to be police, a bank, or the government. Real officials don't demand payment or secrecy over the phone.
tactics.action: Hang up and call the agency back using the number on their official website or your card.

tactics.name: Emotional distress hook
tactics.explanation: This caller is using fear about a loved one to stop you thinking clearly. That panic is the attack.
tactics.action: Take a breath. Hang up and call your loved one directly on their own number to check they're safe.

tactics.name: Isolation / stay-on-the-line
tactics.explanation: This caller won't let you hang up. That's so no one else can warn you it's a scam.
tactics.action: It is always safe to hang up. Do it, then verify through someone you trust.

tactics.name: Credential / verification probe
tactics.explanation: This caller is asking for a code, PIN, or password. Real institutions never ask you to read these out.
tactics.action: Never share codes or passwords by phone. Hang up.

tactics.name: Relationship spoof
tactics.explanation: This caller claims to be family but excuses a strange voice. AI clones sound 'a little off'.
tactics.action: Ask your Watchword — the family safe-word only the real person knows.

fusion.comment: Risk score combines per-tactic confidences, the acoustic synthetic-voice signal, and dangerous combinations. Co-occurrence of money + (urgency|secrecy|distress) is near-certain fraud and is boosted.
